bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and does not yet show meaningful concession, so I will hold firm especially on the high-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.98 because it is still very early at t=0.04 and there is no reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA bid twice, which is extremely poor for me and shows no meaningful concession so far, so I will only make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues D and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering my worst values across every issue. Since issueA and issueB are my lowest-weight issues, I am making a small concession there while holding firm on the more important issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering my worst possible values. To avoid repeating my last bid, I am shifting concession to low-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering all valueA. I will make only a small additional concession on the low-weight issues issueA and issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early (t=0.12), so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA bid without any concession, so I will concede only on low-weight issues A/B while holding firm on the high-weight issues C/D/E.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility by conceding only on the lowest-weight issues while keeping issueC, issueD, and issueE at my best values. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no concession, but it is still early at t=0.14, so I should not make major concessions on my high-importance issues yet. This bid changes my previous offer by moving issueA further toward the opponent’s apparent preference while holding firm elsewhere.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.88 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to concede on high-weight issues yet. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueA bid with no concession, so I will make only a small additional concession on a low-weight issue while holding issueC, issueD, and issueE at my best values.

I targeted a high utility bid around 0.86, conceding further only on the low-weight issues A and B while protecting the high-weight issues D and E plus issue C. Time is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s repeated all-valueA position yet. The opponent has not conceded at all and has sent no messages, so I treat their behavior as firm but not informative enough to justify major concessions.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.20 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA bid without any concession, so I am conceding only on my lowest-weight issues issueA and issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility, since it is still early at t=0.22 but I have already conceded fully on the two lowest-weight issues. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueA bid with no visible concession, so I will make a modest additional concession on issueC while holding firm on the most important issues, issueD and issueE.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76 by conceding further on the medium-weight issueC while protecting the highest-weight issues D and E. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueA bid with no concession, and since time is still early-mid at 0.24, I should continue gradual concessions rather than collapse to their position.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72 by making the next concession on issueC while still protecting the high-weight issues D and E. The opponent has not conceded at all and keeps offering the minimum-value bid, so I should not overreact this early at t=0.26, but I will continue a gradual concession path to improve chances of agreement.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68 by conceding fully on lower-weight issues A and B and now also on medium-weight issue C, while still protecting the highest-weight issues D and E. At t=0.28 there is still time before the deadline, so I should not yet concede on the most important issues. The opponent has not conceded at all and keeps offering all valueA, so I am moving gradually but not accepting their extreme position.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.62, conceding further from my prior bid while still preserving strong value on the highest-importance issues. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to move beyond the previous offer, but not enough to accept the opponent’s repeated all-valueA bid, which gives me very low utility. The opponent has held completely firm with identical offers and no messages, so I am making a measured concession on issueD while keeping issueE at my best value.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.56, continuing concessions only after exhausting lower-weight issues and preserving issueE, one of my highest-weight issues, at valueE. At t=0.32 there is still time, but the opponent has held completely firm at all valueA in every offer, so I am making a controlled concession on issueD rather than dropping everything to their position.

I am targeting roughly the same utility as my last offer (about 0.56) rather than making a large new concession, since time is only 0.34 and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the minimum on every issue. To avoid repeating my prior bid, I am trading within my highest-weight issues by slightly improving issueD for me while conceding issueE one step, testing whether the opponent responds differently.

I targeted about 0.56 utility, maintaining value on the highest-weight issues while still showing a meaningful concession path. At t=0.36 there is time remaining, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s unchanged all-valueA bids; their history shows no concession or preference information beyond holding firm.

I am targeting about 0.50 utility by keeping some value on the two most important issues while conceding all lower-weight issues to the opponent’s apparent preferred valueA. The opponent has not conceded at all and keeps offering all valueA, but at t=0.38 there is still time, so I should not drop all the way to their standing offer yet; this is a further concession from my last bid without repeating it.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.56, keeping concessions mainly on the lower/mid-weight issues while preserving value on the two most important issues, issueD and issueE. The opponent has repeatedly offered only all-valueA with no concession or messages, so I am not treating their stance as informative beyond rigidity; at t=0.40 there is still time to hold above their extreme bid rather than collapse to it.

I am targeting about 0.50 utility: we are in the mid-game and the opponent has shown no concession at all, always repeating the all-valueA bid worth only 0.20 to me. I have already conceded fully on the lower-weight issues and issueC, so I will keep one high-weight issue at valueE while making a further concession on the other high-weight issue to avoid repeating my prior bid.

I targeted about 0.50 utility, keeping meaningful value on the high-weight issues D and E while all low-weight issues A, B, and C have already been conceded. The opponent has held completely firm at all valueA offers with no messages, so at t=0.44 I am making a mid-game tradeoff rather than dropping to their very low-utility bid yet.

I am targeting about 0.50 utility: a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting some value on the two highest-weight issues, issueD and issueE. The opponent has made no numerical concessions at all and keeps offering all valueA, so I am not treating their position as a reliable signal that I must collapse to their bid yet. To avoid repeating my last offer, I am swapping the stronger concession between issueD and issueE while keeping the same overall utility level.

I am targeting about 0.50 utility, continuing the mid-game concession pattern while still protecting the two highest-weight issues where possible. The opponent has repeatedly offered only all valueA with no concession or messages, so I should not drop to their bid yet; instead I will vary the tradeoff on issueD/issueE to avoid repeating my last exact offer.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.44, conceding further because we are halfway through and the opponent has shown no movement from all valueA offers. Since the opponent’s actual offers provide no issue-specific tradeoff signal, I am conceding broadly while still preserving some value on my two highest-weight issues, issueD and issueE, rather than dropping to their all-valueA bid.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.38, conceding further from my prior offer but still preserving some value on the high-importance issues D and E. The opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so at t=0.52 I should move meaningfully but not collapse to their full demand yet.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.38, keeping some value on the high-weight issuesD/issueE while having already conceded fully on issueA, issueB, and issueC. The opponent has held completely firm at all valueA with no messages, so there is no evidence of reciprocal concession; at t=0.54 I will vary my previous bid without dropping to their very low offer yet.

I am targeting a reduced but still positive utility around 0.32, since we are past the midpoint and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering only the all-valueA bid. With no messages and a completely firm opponent pattern, I am conceding further toward their apparent preferred values while preserving some value on the high-weight issueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.38, preserving some value on the high-weight issueD while incorporating the opponent’s first visible concession on issueE to valueB. The opponent has mostly held firm at all valueA, but their latest offer moved issueE to valueB, so I am using that signal while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.32, conceding further from my last offer while still keeping value above the opponent’s latest bid to me. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent has mostly held firm at valueA but recently moved issueE to valueB, so I matched that concession and softened issueD from valueC to valueB. No natural-language messages were provided, so I relied only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a low but positive utility around 0.26 because the opponent has been extremely firm, only moving from all valueA to valueB on issueE after many rounds. At t=0.62, time pressure is meaningful, and since they rejected my prior offer with issueD:valueB and issueE:valueB, I will concede issueD to valueA while keeping their signaled acceptable issueE:valueB to maximize the chance of agreement.
